title: Modular Office Small Desktop
summary: A 52" desktop panel with a trimmed underside and one free-standing leg unit — the other end rests on a matching base cabinet.
description:
This desktop is designed to be one end of a larger desk system: one end of the panel rests on a separate storage base (a narrow open shelf base from the same modular office collection, or any cabinet of matching height), while this build's own leg unit carries the other end. That's why there's only one leg assembly here rather than two — if you're building this as a stand-alone desk with no base cabinet, you'll need to build a second leg unit yourself.

The leg unit is laminated up from three layers of 1x3 rather than built as a hollow frame, which gives it real mass and rigidity in a small footprint. Clamp firmly while gluing, but not so hard you squeeze out all the glue — a joint starved of glue by an overtightened clamp is a real failure mode, not just a theoretical one.

tools.note: Laminates the three-layer leg unit — clamp firmly but not so hard you starve the glue joint.

materials.name: Glued-up 1x panel, 24" wide x 52" long
materials.note: The desktop itself. A wide edge-glued panel, or laminate from narrower 1x stock.

materials.name: 1x3 board, 8 ft
materials.note: You'll actually only need 2 boards plus a 17" scrap — the third board in some shopping lists is a buffer, not a requirement.

steps.title: Build the desktop underside trim
steps.body: Attach the underside trim boards to the desktop with glue and 1-1/4" nails, checking for square as you assemble. If you're staining rather than painting, finish the exposed panel edges with edge banding.

steps.title: Laminate the leg, first two layers
steps.body: Clamp a middle leg layer to an outer leg piece, keeping the bottom and outside edges flush. Glue and 1-1/4" nail, clamping firmly but not so tight you squeeze out all the glue. Let dry. Then add the top apron piece the same way, checking for square as you attach it — and if staining, keep an eye out for glue squeeze-out, which will discolor the wood under a stain.

steps.title: Laminate the leg, final layer
steps.body: Assemble the remaining outer leg piece onto the other side the same way, this time using 2" nails and glue, keeping all outer edges flush. Let dry fully before moving on.

steps.title: Attach the leg unit to the desktop
steps.body: Attach the finished leg unit to the underside of the desktop, positioned at the end that won't rest on a base cabinet. Sand every surface and fill any nail holes before finishing.

images.alt: Small desktop panel with a single laminated leg unit
